source_policy: Derived READ-ONLY from C:\SW_MUSH\data\weapons.yaml and C:\SW_MUSH\docs\design\Guide_01_WEG_D6_Core_Mechanics.md. SW_MUSH is strictly read-only reference; this file is the authoritative project-owned copy. Data flows one way: out of SW_MUSH into data/.
source_note: Ported 2026-06-24 from sw_mush weapons.yaml. Curated subset: Clone Wars-era (20 BBY Mos Eisley) only. Excluded: Heavy Repeating Blaster (E-Web — Imperial doctrine framing). Stormtrooper armor included in armor file only. Faction-issued weapons (dc17, dc15a, e5, officers_sidearm, concealed_blaster) are Clone Wars-era and included with vendor_stocked:false. Contraband weapons retained — they exist in-universe in Clone Wars era. Damage as WEG D6 XD/XD+y pool strings. Ranges in meters [short_min, short_max, medium_max, long_max]. Melee damage uses STR+bonus convention. Range difficulties: point-blank(<short_min)=VE(5), short=E(10), medium=M(15), long=D(20). GEAR EXPANSION (2026-07-03): added 14 more Clone Wars-era weapons mined from previously-unported rows of the same read-only C:\SW_MUSH\data\weapons.yaml (its Drop C/D/E/G melee, blaster, and grenade sections) — thermal_detonator, vibrosaw_greel, stun_gauntlets_ppg, contact_stunner_c512, satskar_ekkar, coynskar_ekkar, flash_pistol_sevari, holdout_blaster_b22, blaster_rifle_firelance, riot_gun_bt500, incendiary_grenade, luma_flare, predator_rifle (contraband), anti_vehicle_grenade (contraband). Data flowed one-way out of the read-only C:\SW_MUSH; nothing there was modified.

hold_out_blaster.name: Hold-Out Blaster
hold_out_blaster.notes: Easily concealed. Illegal or regulated in most systems.

stun_pistol.name: Stun Pistol
stun_pistol.notes: A dedicated stun sidearm. Favored by bail agents and bouncers who need targets breathing.

blaster_carbine.name: Blaster Carbine
blaster_carbine.notes: Rifle punch in a shorter frame; restricted in most systems.

heavy_blaster_pistol.name: Heavy Blaster Pistol
heavy_blaster_pistol.notes: Powerful but shorter range than standard pistols.

sporting_blaster.name: Sporting Blaster
sporting_blaster.notes: Light civilian blaster, common on frontier worlds.

light_repeating_blaster.name: Light Repeating Blaster
light_repeating_blaster.notes: Infantry support weapon. No stun setting.

sporting_blaster_rifle.name: Sporting Blaster Rifle
sporting_blaster_rifle.notes: Hunting rifle. +1D bonus with scope after 1 round aiming.

bowcaster.name: Bowcaster
bowcaster.notes: Wookiee weapon. Requires STR 4D to cock without assistance.

dc17_pistol.name: DC-17 Hand Blaster
dc17_pistol.notes: Republic-issue clone trooper sidearm. Compact, reliable — the standard clone pistol. Faction-issued only; not for sale.

dc15_blaster_rifle.name: DC-15A Blaster Rifle
dc15_blaster_rifle.notes: Standard Republic clone trooper longarm. Extended range for open-field engagements. Faction-issued only.

officers_sidearm.name: Officer's Blaster Pistol
officers_sidearm.notes: DL-44 variant issued to senior officers. Heavy-pistol stopping power in a sidearm frame. Faction-issued only.

e5_blaster_rifle.name: E-5 Blaster Rifle
e5_blaster_rifle.notes: Standard CIS battle rifle, issued to droid infantry and organic CIS operatives. No stun setting. Faction-issued only.

concealed_blaster.name: Concealed Blaster
concealed_blaster.notes: Palm-sized holdout blaster, standard kit for intelligence operatives. Chain-reward only; not for open sale.

heavy_blaster_pistol_t6.name: Heavy Blaster Pistol (Thunderer-pattern)
heavy_blaster_pistol_t6.notes: BlasTech T-6 pattern — hardest-hitting heavy pistol on the lawful market. No stun setting.

blaster_pistol_dl22.name: Blaster Pistol (DL-22)
blaster_pistol_dl22.notes: BlasTech DL-22 — a half-pip hotter than the standard pattern; otherwise the same dependable sidearm.

heavy_blaster_pistol_dl6h.name: Heavy Blaster Pistol (DL-6H)
heavy_blaster_pistol_dl6h.notes: BlasTech DL-6H — a heavy that keeps standard-pistol range.

sniper_rifle_x45.name: Sniper Rifle (X-45)
sniper_rifle_x45.notes: SoroSuub X-45 — integral scope steadies long-range work.

disruptor_pistol.name: Disruptor Pistol
disruptor_pistol.notes: Banned on every world with a functioning court. Possession alone is an infraction; patrols confiscate on sight.

force_pike.name: Force Pike
force_pike.notes: Energy polearm favored by ceremonial and elite guards.

gaderffii.name: Gaderffii (Gaffi Stick)
gaderffii.notes: Tusken Raider weapon. Clone Wars-era Tatooine locals know this weapon well.

vibrodagger_talon.name: Vibrodagger (Talon-pattern)
vibrodagger_talon.notes: LaserHone Talon — ultrasonic edge, near-silent. The fringe's favorite boot knife.

vibrorapier_duelist.name: Vibrorapier (Duelist-pattern)
vibrorapier_duelist.notes: LaserHone Duelist — ultrasonic edge, near-silent. A boarding-action favorite.

lightsaber.name: Lightsaber
lightsaber.notes: If attacker misses difficulty by 10+, hits self with full damage. Requires Force sensitivity. Cannot be purchased.

stun_grenade.name: Stun Grenade
stun_grenade.notes: Merr-Sonn riot pattern — rechargeable and reusable. Stun-only by design.

autocaster_drolan.name: Auto-caster
autocaster_drolan.notes: Drolan Plasteel repeating crossbow — auto-loads the next quarrel. Silent, cheap to feed, beats energy scanners.

thermal_detonator.name: Thermal Detonator
thermal_detonator.notes: Class A restricted ordnance. Possession alone is a serious offense on any world with a functioning court.

vibrosaw_greel.name: Vibro-saw
vibrosaw_greel.notes: Greel Wood Logging industrial cutter. A legal tool that happens to go through bulkheads — heavy, loud, unsubtle.

stun_gauntlets_ppg.name: Stun Gauntlets
stun_gauntlets_ppg.notes: Palandrix PPG — charged knuckle plates, ten discharges per power cell. Hits land as electrified impact.

contact_stunner_c512.name: Contact Stunner
contact_stunner_c512.notes: SoroSuub C5-12 Stun Master — palm-press stun device on a standard energy cell (15 charges). Favored by skip tracers; its anti-detector shielding is lore until a scan-defeat mechanic exists.

satskar_ekkar.name: Sat'skar
satskar_ekkar.notes: Ekkar Arms — the Coynite greatsword. Brutal two-handed work; one-handed use drops it to STR+1D (book rule, kept as lore until a grip mechanic exists).

coynskar_ekkar.name: Coyn'skar
coynskar_ekkar.notes: Ekkar Arms bladed pole — a Coynite honor weapon. The hook-disarm trick (Very Difficult) stays book-lore until a disarm-maneuver consumer exists.

flash_pistol_sevari.name: Sevari Flash-Pistol
flash_pistol_sevari.notes: Hand-finished single-shot archaic pistol with an integral blade (STR+1D, book-lore). Misfires on a wild 1 in the book — flavor until a misfire mechanic exists.

holdout_blaster_b22.name: Hold-out Blaster (B22)
holdout_blaster_b22.notes: Merr-Sonn B22 — palm-sized insurance. Short legs, easy to hide. A slightly weaker cousin of the standard hold-out blaster.

blaster_rifle_firelance.name: Blaster Rifle (Firelance)
blaster_rifle_firelance.notes: SoroSuub Firelance — a production-line rifle with the serials conveniently absent. The hunter's gun that doesn't ask questions.

riot_gun_bt500.name: Riot Gun
riot_gun_bt500.notes: BlasTech 500 — a 300-shot drum built for sustained suppression. Constant-fire rules stay book-lore until a suppression mechanic exists.

incendiary_grenade.name: Incendiary Grenade
incendiary_grenade.notes: Greff-Timms 0033X — Old Republic jump-trooper issue, still in production. Burns hot and clings.

luma_flare.name: Luma Flare
luma_flare.notes: Salamini Model-3287 — a signal flare that doubles as a weapon in a pinch: 4D burn on a direct hit. Book: 300 m illumination for 3 minutes, blinds within 50 m — lore until light and blind mechanics exist.

predator_rifle.name: Predator Rifle (EXP-7a)
predator_rifle.notes: Exotac's prototype hunting rifle. Recoil the book gates behind a Moderate Strength roll (lore until a recoil mechanic exists). Never went to lawful market.

anti_vehicle_grenade.name: Anti-Vehicle Grenade
anti_vehicle_grenade.notes: Galentro Armaments shaped-charge grenade. The book waives scale reduction against speeder-class vehicles — lore until vehicle-scale combat exists. Restricted everywhere.
